response.justification: The candidate brings a genuinely relevant combination of geospatial data platform experience (founder of GISCollective, GIS work for Green Map, FOSS4G speaker) and high-performance systems work (low-latency, async I/O microservices handling millions of requests per second at Dunnhumby/Sociomantic). However, that systems work was in DLang rather than modern C++ or Rust, and his direct C/C++ experience is brief and dated (National Instruments, 2014–2015). Recent roles are TypeScript/Node full-stack product work, and there is no direct routing or navigation guidance experience. Strong engineering fundamentals and domain adjacency make him worth considering, but the core language requirement is a real gap.
strengths: Deep geospatial domain experience: founded and maintains an open-source Geo-Data platform used in production
Proven low-latency distributed systems work (async I/O, caching, fibers) at very high request volumes
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, testing tools authorship, Linux and Kubernetes operations
concerns: Modern C++ experience is minimal and dates back to 2014–2015; no Rust experience listed
No direct routing engine, map tiling, or navigation guidance experience
Recent years focused on TypeScript/Node product work, away from systems-level programming
